task_desc: Fix education panel VNC/Motor neuron group count bug and "~70 groups" text inconsistency with actual 59-group connectome -- Two user-visible data display bugs in education.js: (1) The VNC/Motor region population line at education.js:171 displays "6 neuron groups representing ~15,075 real neurons" but should display "17 neuron groups representing ~15,075 real neurons"; the population total is computed correctly by iterating the base 6 neurons plus 11 dynamically-collected MN_ prefix neurons (lines 156-168), but the group count uses region.neurons.length which only reflects the static EDUCATION_REGIONS array (6 entries) and does not include the MN_ prefix neurons; brain3d.js handles this correctly because _buildRegions() pushes MN_ neurons into the resolved neuron list at build time; fix by counting the MN_ neurons during the population sum loop and adding them to the displayed group count, (2) The education panel intro at education.js:131 says "Our model compresses this into ~70 functional neuron groups" but the connectome has exactly 59 groups (verified: Object.keys(BRAIN.connectome).length === 59 and Object.keys(neuronPopulations).length === 59), and the T6.3 connectome summary header displays "59 groups / ~130K neurons"; users see "~70" in the Learn panel and "59" in the connectome header simultaneously, which is contradictory; similarly the "What's Missing" section at education.js:213 says "Our 70-group model" which should also be corrected to match the actual count. Files: js/education.js.
